puzzle.title: The Named One in the Prophet's Household
puzzle.intro: In the household of the final messenger, there lived a soul whose personal story became a divine lesson for all believers. This figure's life was intimately connected to a transformative command that reshaped social customs forever, marking a profound shift in how faith would define family and belonging.
clues: My name was once linked to a noble lineage, but a higher truth would later clarify the true bonds of faith.
I was the recipient of great favor from Allah and His messenger, a favor that was publicly declared in the Book.
My marital life became a pivotal test that would forever change the perception of adopted relationships.
I was the one whose personal trial led to a divine command that allowed the Prophet to marry a woman after I departed.
Through my story, the distinction between biological and chosen family was made clear for all generations.
I am the only one among my peers whose name appears explicitly in the last revelation, not as a prophet, but as a servant tested and honored.

outcome.label: Outcome
options: The abolition of adoption as a means of creating blood relations
The establishment of the first Islamic state
outcome.answer: The abolition of adoption as a means of creating blood relations

puzzle.arabic: وَإِذْ تَقُولُ لِلَّذِىٓ أَنْعَمَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَأَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيْهِ أَمْسِكْ عَلَيْكَ زَوْجَكَ وَٱتَّقِ ٱللَّهَ وَتُخْفِى فِى نَفْسِكَ مَا ٱللَّهُ مُبْدِيهِ وَتَخْشَى ٱلنَّاسَ وَٱللَّهُ أَحَقُّ أَن تَخْشَىٰهُ ۖ فَلَمَّا قَضَىٰ زَيْدٌۭ مِّنْهَا وَطَرًۭا زَوَّجْنَـٰكَهَا لِكَىْ لَا يَكُونَ عَلَى ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ حَرَجٌۭ فِىٓ أَزْوَٰجِ أَدْعِيَآئِهِمْ إِذَا قَضَوْا۟ مِنْهُنَّ وَطَرًۭا ۚ وَكَانَ أَمْرُ ٱللَّهِ مَفْعُولًۭا
puzzle.verse: And [remember, O Muḥammad], when you said to the one on whom Allāh bestowed favor and you bestowed favor, "Keep your wife and fear Allāh," while you concealed within yourself that which Allāh is to disclose. And you feared the people, while Allāh has more right that you fear Him. So when Zayd had no longer any need for her, We married her to you in order that there not be upon the believers any discomfort [i.e., guilt] concerning the wives of their claimed [i.e., adopted] sons when they no longer have need of them. And ever is the command [i.e., decree] of Allāh accomplished. (33:37)
